body.title: BG lateral inhibition: move _inhibit_neighbors() to score-time not post-selection
body.result: Added get_inhibited_habit_ids() to cortex.py (reads bg_inhibition edges targeting candidates). select_habit() now preloads the inhibited set before the scoring loop and applies a graded -0.15 penalty during scoring. _inhibit_neighbors() stays post-selection to write new edges after each winner. This makes lateral inhibition biologically correct: near-miss candidates enter the next competition already penalised, so the dominant habit wins consistently.
body.description: basal_ganglia.py _inhibit_neighbors() fires AFTER the winner is selected (line 735), not during the competition scoring phase. Biological lateral inhibition narrows the competition space — it should suppress near-miss candidates DURING scoring so the winner emerges from a filtered pool. Fix: call _inhibit_neighbors() inside _score_habit() or before winner selection in select_habit(), not after. This unlocks proper attentional filtering and is the #2 liveness finding from deep-audit 2026-04-04.
